prevalence.trend: stable; higher in women and older adults

incidence.source: Primary care and GI cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high QOL impact and healthcare utilization

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV functional constipation criteria; rule out alarm features and secondary causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with diet, laxatives, and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; limited access to specialist motility testing
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary fiber and fluids, osmotic laxatives (PEG/lactulose), stimulant laxatives short-term; evaluate secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low dietary fiber intake and inconsistent long-term follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost treatments available through primary care and SUS
